def: s.f. The sound made in spitting, or an imitation of that sound; — intj. (expressive of disgust, scorn, &c.) Fie!, out!, tush!, tut!
subs: v.n. To be constantly or repeatedly spitting; to spit (on or at, -par) in scorn, &c.; to flout or jeer with the exclamation thū; to deride, scorn, treat with disdain
v.n. To be flouted or jeered with the exclamation thū; to be derided, scorned, &c.; to be disgraced.

def: s.m. A heap, mound; clod, lump (of clay); a mound or rude pillar of mud (used to mark the boundary of a field; syn. ḍhūʼā).
s.m. = thūhar, q.v.
s.m. = thūhar, q.v.
s.m. = thūhar, q.v.
s.f. The chin; — parched grain; — a spickle or head of Indian corn and the like, after the grains have been picked out, a cob
subs: (or ṭhuḍḍī) pakaṛnā (-kī), lit. 'To take hold of the chin'; to chuck under the chin; to humour, to flatter, to curry favour (with); to coax; to beg; to mollify; to appease
s.m. The dimple in a chin.

def: s.f. The chin; — parched grain; — a spickle or head of Indian corn and the like, after the grains have been picked out, a cob
subs: (or ṭhuḍḍī) pakaṛnā (-kī), lit. 'To take hold of the chin'; to chuck under the chin; to humour, to flatter, to curry favour (with); to coax; to beg; to mollify; to appease
s.m. The dimple in a chin.

def: s.m. = thūʼā, q.v.
s.m. A species of thorny Euphorbia, a cactus, Euphorbia neriifolia, or E. antiquorum (often written thūʼar; syn. sīj; ti-dhārā; and zaqqūm)
subs: s.m. The Euphorbia with flat oval joints (the peculiar habitat of the cochineal insect)

def: s.m. Spittle, saliva (cf. thū); the gossamer
subs: v.n. lit. 'To churn spittle'; to chatter nonsense, to talk idly
v.t. To treat with sovereign contempt (see next)
(-par), To apply spittle (to), (also a term of abuse and most indecent meaning); to spit (upon, in contempt), to defeat, overcome
intj. Fie!, for shame!, pshaw!, pish!, faugh!, pooh!.

def: adj. & s.m. = thukhāʼī, q.v.
v.t. To cause to spit, to make (one) spit; to cause to reproach or despise, &c.
adj. & s.m. Fit to be spit upon, vile, base, contemptible
s.m. Spittle = thūk, q.v. (used chiefly in comp.)
subs: s.f. Disgrace; abuse; strife, contention, quarrel

ety: thūk˚ = Prk. थुक्क(इ) or थुक्के(इ) = S. ष्ठेव or स्थेव+कृ
def: v.n. To spit (at, -meṅ or -par), to treat with utter contempt; to vilify, abuse
subs: v.t. (intens. of thūknā), To spit out; to give up (in disgust, &c.), to leave, abandon
or thūk ćāṭnā, v.n. To swallow one's own words, to retract or recall a promise, to break one's word

def: v.n. To be knocked, hammered, beaten; to be punished or chastised; to be defeated, overcome, worsted; to be thrown on the back (in wrestling); to suffer a loss; to be put in the stocks; to be imprisoned
s.m. corr. of ṭukṛā, q.v.

ety: ṭhukrā˚ = ṭhokar, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To kick against, to strike the foot against ( = ṭhokar mārnā); to kick, trample upon; to spurn.
v.n. To spit (especially on hearing the name of a disease for the purpose of warding it off, or for the purpose of averting an evil eye).
v.t. To cause to be beaten, or hammered, &c.
adv. Falling gently, dropping, trickling (as water from a small height; — cf. ćul-ćul-ānā).
adj. Of low stature, stunted, stumpy, short, diminutive; dapper
subs: s.f. Mincing gait or air, affected walk; stately or dignified gait, strut.

def: v.n. To walk with a jerky, mincing, or wanton gait; to walk affectedly; to walk with grace, stateliness, or dignity; to strut, stalk.
s.f. = ṭhumrī, q.v.
adj. Of low stature, stunted, stumpy, short, diminutive; dapper
subs: s.f. Mincing gait or air, affected walk; stately or dignified gait, strut.

def: v.t. To cause to move or walk with stateliness, dignity, or grace; to cause to strut; to give a jerk to, to jerk (a paper kite); to make stunted; to hammer or beat down or in.
adj. & s.m. Idle, lazy; — a lazy fellow.
s.f. Jerking or playing (a paper kite, to keep it up when the wind is light)
subs: ṭhumkī lagānā (-ko), To give a jerk (to), to jerk (a paper kite) = ṭhumkānā.

def: adj. Small, short, &c. ( = ṭhumkā).
s.f. A species of metre; a variety of song, a species of amorous composition.

ety: S. स्तोम or स्थूणा (with व inserted)+कः
def: s.m. A lump of earth (put on the end of a lever as a balance).
v.t. To strike with the beak, to peck, &c. = ṭūṅgnā; ṭhuṅgernā, qq.v.
s.f. Pecking; picking, eating squeamishly, or bit by bit ( = ṭungār, q.v.; c.f. also ṭūṅg and ṭhoṅg).
v.t. To peck; to pick, to eat squeamishly, to piddle.
v.t. To strike with the beak, to peck, &c. = ṭūṅgnā; ṭhuṅgernā, qq.v.
s.f. (local) = thūnī, q.v.
s.f. Pillar, column, post, prop, the upright post over the mouth of a well.
v.t. = ṭhukwānā, q.v.

ety: caus. and doub. caus. of ṭhūṅsnā
def: v.t. = ṭhusānā; ṭhuswānā, q.v.

ety: ṭhoṅs˚ or ṭhos˚ = Prk. टुच्छ(इ) = S. त्वक्ष(ति)
def: v.t. To press down (or in) firm or hard, to beat (or drive, or force) in, to stuff, cram, thrust in; to wad; to eat greedily, to devour

ety: caus. and doub. caus. of ṭhūṅsnā
def: v.t. = ṭhusānā; ṭhuswānā, q.v.
s.m. = ṭhūṅṭh, q.v.
s.m. Stump (of an arm, branch, &c.), branch (of a tree) broken off at the end and leafless; an amputated hand or arm; a stunted tree.
adj. Reduced to a stump, having the hand (or arm, &c.) amputated; having its branches lopped and leafless (a tree).
s.f. A small stump; a stalk; stubble.
adj. Having the hand, or arm, &c. amputated; mutilated ( = ṭhūṅṭhā, q.v.); — s.m. A eunuch.
adj. Having the hand, or arm, &c. amputated; mutilated ( = ṭhūṅṭhā, q.v.); — s.m. A eunuch.
s.m. The post which acts as a support to the ḍheklī.

ety: caus. of thopnā, q.v.; — thupā˚ = Prk. थुप्पावे˚ = S. स्तूप्य˚, rt. स्तूप्+आपि
def: v.t. To cause to prop, &c.; to cause to be propped, &c.
v.t. To bear, endure, suffer.
adj. Faint-hearted, cowardly; narrow-hearted, miserly, close.
s.f. Scarcity, lack.
s.f. Parched grain ( = ṭhuḍḍī, q.v.).

r: thūṛī, thoṛī, thū, ṛī, ṛe, aṛe
ety: thū, q.v.+ṛī, fem. of ṛe or aṛe, q.v.
def: intj. Ah!, fie!, for shame! (see thuṛī).
s.f. Spitting (at or on, -par, in scorn); disgrace, dishonour, shame, infamy (used chiefly by women); — intj. (express. of disgust, &c.), Ah! fie! for shame!
v.t. To strike, beat, injure, ruin, destroy, consume, devour.
v.n. To fall short, be insufficient; to be wanting, to fail, be lacking
s.f. (prov.) = ṭhuḍḍī, q.v.

ety: ṭhusak˚ = Prk. टुच्छक्के(इ), fr. S. त्वक्ष+कृ
def: v.n. To weep silently or softly; to sob ( = ṭusaknā); peditum ciere, suppedere.
v.t. = ṭhuswānā, q.v.
s.f. Ventris crepitus surdus.
v.n. To be filled full, to be stuffed, or crammed.
v.t. To cause to stuff or cram; to have (one) stuffed or crammed (with).

ety: thoth+nā or nī = Prk. डओ or डिआ = S. ल+कः or इका
def: = H تهوتهنا थोथना thothnā, थुथना thuthnā, s.m. = H تهوتهني थोथनी thothnī, थुथनीthuthnī, s.f. The mouth or snout (of an animal, as a horse, camel, or hog, &c).
v.n. To distort (the mouth), to hang or turn down the lip (in disgust, &c.), to pout; to turn away (from, in disgust, &c.); to frown, scowl
subs: To make a wry face, to pout, &c.

def: s.f. = thūthnī or thothnī, q.v.
s.m. The mouth or snout (of an animal); the mouth or face (of a human being: see thoth).
s.m. Making the sound thut in spitting; sputtering, spitting; — cholera morbus (in the language of Mohammadan women).
s.f. A sputtering hag; a hag, witch
subs: (for the leg); a shoe, slipper (local, used chiefly by women).

def: v.n. To spit, to sputter; to spit or hiss (as a snake, &c.); to spit (at), to drive away scornfully; — to imitate the sound and action of spitting (in order to avert evil from one)
subs: v.t. To make the sound thut, as of spitting, over (a person or thing, in order to avert evil).

def: s.m. = thuthṛā, q.v.
adj. Three (common in Braj; but in Hindī and Urdū used chiefly as first member of compounds, e.g. ti-bārā, 'thrice'; ti-guṇ, 'three-fold').
adj. Three (in comp. e.g. ti-bārā, q.v.).
s.f. = tīyā, q.v.

ety: Prk. तेहि, तेवहि, तिवहि (hi being the gen. or obl. suff., and omitted in Hindī); S. तावत्
def: Inflected bases (in Braj) of the sing. of the pers. pron. of the third person, corresponding to the Hindī us; tis; 'him, her, it' (e.g. tā-kau = us-kā; tā-kī = tis-kī; tā-te or tā-teṅ = tis-se).

ety: inf. n. of طيب 'to be good, or pleasant,' &c.
def: s.m. A fragrant substance, a perfume, odour; — the most excellent (of any sort or thing), the essence, the cream (of).
s.m. = ṭibbā; ṭilā, qq.v.

ety: for A. طبابة, inf. n. of طبّ
def: s.f. The medical art, the practice of physic.
adj. & s.m. = تباره tibāra, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Three times, thrice; — a hall or room with three doors.
s.f. = tibāra.
adj. Thrice stale, three days old (food).

ety: inf. n. of طبّ 'to treat medically'
def: s.f. Medical treatment, medicine; — fascination, enchantment, magic.
s.m. A height, rising ground, hill ( = ṭīlā); a sand-bank, a shoal (in the sea).
s.m. = ṭibbā, q.v.
adj. Medical, therapeutical, medicinal

ety: tibh˚ = S. स्तेप(ते), rt. स्तिप+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To give out in driblets; to make (one) a small daily allowance (to subsist on); to excite desire, or cupidity ( = lālać denā).
s.m. A small daily allowance
subs: (-ko), To give (one) a small daily allowance ( = ṭibhānā).

ety: anom. inf. n. ii of بين 'to become separated'
def: s.f. Making manifest, manifestation, &c. (see bayān).
adj. = tīkshṇa, q.v.
adj. = tīkshṇa, tīkhā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Having three doors, three-doored: — a house with three doors; a frame for three doors in a row.
adj. Three-doored, &c. = tidarā, q.v.
s.m. = ṭiḍḍā (the usual form), q.v.

ety: S. त्रि+ष्ठ+कः; lit. 'Having three supports or joints' in each foot
def: s.m. A grasshopper (cf. ṭiḍḍī).
s.f. A locust ( = ṭīṛī)
subs: s.m. A host or cloud of locusts.

def: s.f.; prop. ṭiḍḍī, q.v.

ety: Prk. तेद्दहरे (re being the loc. affix); S. तावत्
def: adv. There, thither, in that direction (correl. of jidhar).
s.m. lit. 'Having three edges'; the plants Euphorbia antiquorum, and Scirpus kysoor; — the meeting of three streams (syn. tir-benī).

ety: the preceding, with S.इकः and इका instead of कः
def: adj. & s.f. Having three lines, streams, or edges; — three streams or lines
s.f. = ṭiḍḍī, q.v.
s.m. = ṭiṭṭibh, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. طفل 'to be soft or tender,' &c.
def: s.m. An infant, a child; young (of an animal), youngling
subs: s.m. A school-boy; a tyro, a raw, inexperienced person.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طفل (tifl), from Arabic طِفْل (ṭifl).

ety: t̤ifl, q.v.+Zend aff. āna, or ana = S. अन
def: adj. Like a child, childish, infantine.
s.f. State or condition of an infant, or of a child; infancy; childhood.
s.m. An errand-boy.
s.f. Giving a back, taking pickback (used by boys).
v.n. To be fused or melted, to melt, dissolve; to rarefy.
v.t. To melt, to dissolve ( = pighlānā).
adj. & s.m. Sharp, pointed (as a weapon, &c.); hot, pungent, acrid; violent, fiery, passionate, hasty; — a hot or pungent flavour; heat, pungency
adj. & adv. Threefold, treble, triple; thrice; — tiguṇ, s.m. A tone in music.
adj. & adv. Threefold, treble, triple; thrice; — tiguṇ, s.m. A tone in music.
adj. Empty, &c. = tihī, q.v.

ety: Prk. तइआइआ; तृतीय+ता+इका with euphonic h
def: s.f. A third part; the third part of a piece of cloth for making trousers.
s.f. The spleen (syn. tillī).
s.f. Cotton pods the fibre of which equals one-third of the whole produce.
adj. (Braj, or old H.) = tumhārā.

ety: S. त्रयस्+कार+कः; or perhaps fr. tihāṛnā = tihrānā, q.v.
def: s.m. Division into three equal parts; a third part; — tihāṛā, s.m. or tihāṛe-kī baṭāʼī, s.f. A division of the produce into three equal parts, one of which goes to the proprietor of the land, and the other two to the cultivators.
adj. (Braj, or old H.) = tumhārā.
adj. Seventy-three.
s.m. = tisrāyat, tisrait, qq.v.
adj. Empty, void, vacant, vain
(Braj) = us-ko bhī; usī-ko.
adj. Three; the three ( = tīnoṅ, q.v.).
adv. (Braj) = tihuṅ, q.v.

s.f. The third day of a lunar fortnight; a festival held on the third day of the lunar fortnight of the month Sāwan; — the red-velvet insect ( = bīr-bahuṭṭī), the lady-bird (so called, probably, because of its appearance in Bhādoṇ)
subs: s.m. A festival; — presents suitable on the occasion of a festival ( = tīr-teʼohār).

def: adj. & s.m. Third (in number or degree); — a third a third one; the third day after the death of a relation (on which oblations are offered by Mohammadans); the offerings made on this occasion.

ety: for A. تجارة; quasi-inf. n. of تجر 'to trade'
def: s.f. The practice of traffic, traffic, trade, commerce; merchandise
subs: s.f. Place of traffic or commerce; — tijārat-gāh-ě-ʻālī, s.f. Emporium (syn. baṛī manḍī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تِجَارَت (tijārat), from Arabic تِجَارَة (tijāra).

def: adj. Commercial, mercantile.
s.f. Tertian ague, remittent fever occurring every third day.
s.f. = tijārī, q.v.
s.f. An ornament (as a gold patch, &c.), worn on the neck or head; (local) a top-knot; a drop, line, or stream (of blood, &c.).

s.m. Small round mark (or marks) made on the forehead and between the eyebrows (of coloured earth or unguents, and intended either for ornament or for sectarial distinction (the mark is commonly made at the ceremony of betrothal, or at that of installation to an office, &c.; or when one is setting out on a journey, or a pilgrimage, or is about to undertake an enterprise); an ornamental patch of gold, or silver, or tinsel, &c., or a jewel, worn on the forehead; a small round mark or patch made on any part of the body; a stain, blot; inoculation, vaccination; the ceremony connected with betrothal, or installation or investiture (see above); dowry; nuptial gifts, presents in money or goods from the bride's father to the bridegroom
subs: (-ko), To send the nuptial gifts (to the bridegroom)
(-se), To receive or accept the nuptial gifts.

def: s.f. A commentary, annotation, gloss

ety: ṭīkā+Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन् termin. of pres. part. with augment. आपि
def: adj. Invested with the ṭīkā or badge of sovereignty, inaugurated, installed.

v.t. To cause to stop, stay, remain, or last, &c.; to stop, detain, check; to retain; to fix (in or on a place), to station, encamp; to put (one) up, lodge, billet; to apply, lay on, give (a blow or slap, &c.)
s.f. A prop for the support of the shafts or pole of a carriage (cf. tek).

ety: rt. of ṭikānā+āʼo = Prk. इअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Stability, permanence, durability, firmness; residence, abode.
s.m. Three ploughings (cf. tikhrā).
s.m. = tikār, q.v.
s.f. A kind of soil which is irretentive of moisture.
s.m. A tax, toll, duty, income-tax, house-tax.
s.m. Halting-place, resting-place, caravansary.
s.f. A ticket (railway, &c.), a postage-stamp; a visiting card; a pass, passport; a label; a badge
Buttocks, posteriors.
Buttocks, posteriors.
s.f. A three-footed stool; a small three-legged table, a teapoy.

ety: ṭikā, rt. of ṭikānā +ū, Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. Stationary, fixed, firm, stable, durable, lasting.

adj. Sharp, hot, pungent, &c. ( = tīkshṇa, q.v. & cf. tez); piercing, keen; bright, sparkling; comely, graceful; piquant, smart, lively sprightly; spruce, dapper; spicy, racy; fine, shrill, high (as a sound or tone); — s.m. A comely or beautiful person
subs: s.f. A sharp tone, a shrill or high note

def: v.t. = ṭikhārnā, q.v.
v.t. To plough three times before sowing, to trifallow; — to inquire closely or earnestly into; to establish, confirm, prove (syn. sthir karnā; ṭhaḥrānā).
v.n. To cry or call (as a partridge); to sing or warble sweetly.
part. Ploughed three times; — s.m. Ploughing three times
subs: v.t. To plough three times before sowing, to trifallow.

def: s.f. = tir-khūṅṭ, q.v.
s.f. A starchy substance obtained from the root of the Curcuma angusti folia; Indian arrowroot.
s.f. A small cake of bread, a wafer ( = ṭikkī); a small cake of charcoal for a ḥuqqa; a bolus.
s.m. See تكه tikka.
s.m. A bit, piece, a mouthful; a small piece of flesh, a lump of meat, a slice, a stake, chop, collop
subs: (-kā), To cut in pieces, to slice; to make mince-meat (of).

def: s.m. (f.?) A thick cake of bread (cf. ṭikṛī, ṭiklī).
s.f. A small cake of bread; livelihood, scanty means of subsistence; (fig.) interest, influence ( = laggā)
subs: v.n. To place cakes (in an oven, or on a baking-pan), to bake bread; to earn a scanty livelihood; to make interest, form a connexion.

def: s.f. A small round cake, a wafer; an ornament worn on the forehead, a spangle (cf. ṭipkā, ṭīkā); a small round piece of metal (to close a hole, &c.).
v.t. To aim at, &c. = tāknā; taknā; qq.v.
v.n. To stop, stay, remain, tarry; to halt, put up, lodge, abide; to be detained; to stick fast (in); to last, endure, continue
adj. & s.m. Three-cornered, triangular; — a triangle.
adj. & s.m. Three-cornered, triangular; — a triangle.
adj. = tikon.
s.m. = ṭikorā, q.v.
s.m. A small unripe mango (syn. kairī).
s.f. = ṭikṛī, q.v.
s.f. Spreading hog-weed, Boerhavia procumbens or diffusa.
s.f. = ṭiklī, q.v.
s.f. A small field of inferior land (cf. ṭhīkrā).
adj. = tīkshna, q.v.
adj. Sharp (in all senses), hot, pungent, warm, fiery; acid; fine, thin; ardent, zealous, active; quick, keen, intelligent, shrewd, penetrating, subtle; harsh, rough, angry, passionate (syn. tez)
subs: adj. & s.f. Sharp-witted, sagacious, acute, shrewd, witty; — acuteness of intellect, sagacity, &c.
s.m. lit. 'Having a pungent flower'; cloves
s.m. 'Having pungent fruit'; coriander; black mustard
s.m. 'Pungent oil'; the resin of the Shorea robusta; resin; the milky juice of the Euphorbia lactea; spirituous or vinous liquor
adj. 'Sharp in action,' active, brisk, energetic, zealous
s.m. 'Having a pungent smell'; the plant Morunga hyperanthera; the gum-olibanum tree; — small cardamoms
s.f. Name of several plants; mustard-seed; orris root; the plant Pandanus odoratissimus; &c.

def: s.f. = S تيکشنتو तीक्ष्णत्व tīkshṇatva, s.m. Warmth, heat; sharpness, pungency; harshness, roughness; subtlety, acuteness, keenness, shrewdness; fineness, &c. (cf. tezī).
s.m. = S تيکشنتا तीक्ष्णता tīkshṇatā, s.f. Warmth, heat; sharpness, pungency; harshness, roughness; subtlety, acuteness, keenness, shrewdness; fineness, &c. (cf. tezī).
adj. & s.m. Bitter, pungent; fragrant (see tītā); — bitter taste, bitterness, pungency; fragrance
subs: s.m. A kind of cucumber, Trichosanthes dioica
s.m. The cucurbitaceous plant Momordia mixta
s.f. The pot-herb Hilancha repens; the plant Menispermum glabrum
s.f. A medicinal kind of moon plant; name of several other plants

def: s.m. A kind of gourd, Trichosanthes diœca; — a sort of gentian, Gentian cherayta.
s.f. contrac. of ṭikṭikī, 'stare,' q.v.
s.f. The sound uttered by the driver of a cart to urge on the bullocks, &c. drawing it.
s.f. A bitter gourd.
s.f. A small triangular frame, a triangle; a tripod.

ety: fr. S. दृक् redupl. or ṭak redupl.
def: s.f. Fixed look, steadfast gaze, stare ( = tak, tāk, q.v.)
subs: (or ṭikṭikī) bāṅdhnā or lagānā (-par), To regard with a fixed look, to gaze steadfastly (at), to stare (at); to stand or continue gazing or peeping (at)
v.n. To remain staring or wide open (the eyes).

ety: ṭik-ṭik, imit. of the sound uttered by the animal
def: s.f. The common house-lizard (of India).
s.f. = tīkhur, q.v.

s.m. The sesamum plant, Sesamum indicum; the seed of the sesamum, oil-seed; a black spot or speck (as the focus of solar rays through a burning glass); a mole or a black spot (on the face, compared to a seed of sesamum); the pupil of the eye; a small particle or portion, the least bit (as much as a sesamum-seed); a moment or instant; — a kind of sweetmeat ( = til-kuṭ; til-kī miṭhāʼī)
subs: tilānjalī (tila+anjalī), s.f. A handful (or a double handful) of water mixed with sesamum-seeds offered to the manes of deceased ancestors
(-ko), To make an oblation of water and sesamum-seeds, to perform funeral obsequies
v.n. A black spot or speck to form (as that of the sun's rays through a burning-glass)
s.m. A sort of sweetmeat made chiefly of ground sesamum
s.f. The sandal tree, Pterocarpus santalinus; sandal-wood, red sanders
til-pej, s.m. Barren sesamum (which bears no blossom, or whose seed yields no oil)
adv. By little and little, by degrees, slowly; — til-til-kā ḥisāb, or til-til-kī lekhā, An account of every particle or fraction, a strict or exact account
til-ćāʼolī, s.f. A mixture composed of rice and sesamum-seeds; a mixture of black and grey (as in the hair of the head or beard, &c.)
(from ćūrṇa), s.f. A kind of sweetmeat (cf. til-piććaṭ; til-kuṭ)
s.f. Sesamum made up in the shape of a cow for the purpose of being presented to Brāhmans
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat made of sesamum and sugar
s.m. A mole or natural spot on the body; a man so marked
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat composed of pounded sesamum and guṛ or sugar
adj. Made of sesamum, consisting of sesamum; abounding in sesamum
s.m. A species of pea-fowl (marked with spots like sesamum-seed)
(˚la+od˚), s.m. A dish of milk, rice, and sesamum.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit तिल (tila), from Sanskrit तिल (tila), borrowed from a Munda language.

def: s.f. A small fowl, a young hen, a pullet; (contempt.) a fat young woman.
s.m. Drawn gold, &c. = tilā or tillā, q.v.
s.m. Mound, hillock, rising ground, hill ( = ṭekrā).

ety: v.n. fr. طلي 'to daub, besmear, anoint,' &c.
def: s.m. Liniment, unguent, plaster, ointment, embrocation, wash, &c.

ety: prob. for orig. تلا tilā, or تله tila; but cf. next
def: s.m. Gold, red gold; gold fringe
subs: s.f. Gilding; — the trade of a gilder.

ety: Prk. तिलओ; S. तिलकः; cf. P. tila, and P. & A. t̤ilā
def: s.m. Ointment, salve; overlay; drawn gold; an ornamental fringe, gold-lace border (of a turban, &c.)
subs: tile-dānī, s.f. A small bag for holding needle, thread, scissors, &c.; a housewife, a hold-all.

def: v.n. = talaphnā, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. tilā.

r: tilāʼī, tilā˚, tilānā, tel, āʼī, talāʼī
ety: tilā˚, rt. of tilānā, fr. tel, q.v.+āʼī; see talāʼī
def: s.f. Oiling; pouring oil on (a torch, &c.).
adj. Golden; made of gold; covered with gold
subs: s.f. Painting or lettering with gold.

def: s.m. A turtle dove.
s.f. The day after the dulhainḍī or second day of the Holī festival.

s.m. Ornament, the ornament of anything (used in comp. to express greatness or distinction, e.g. trailokya-tilak, 'the ornament of the three worlds,' i.e. a distinguished personage); an ornament or other mark between the eyes or rising between the brows; a sectarial mark or marks made with coloured eye-earths, sandal wood, or unguents upon the forehead and between the eyebrows (as a ceremonial mark it is made on the occasions of installation to office, coronation of a king, betrothal, &c., and hence is sometimes synonymous with 'installation,' 'consecration,' &c.); a freckle, mole, natural mark under the skin ( = til); a species of tree with beautiful flowers; a commentary ( = ṭīkā)
subs: (-par), tilak dhārnā, tilak dhāran karnā, To form the tilak mark on the forehead, to ornament, to wear the tilak
s.m. One who is marked with the tilak, one who wears the tilak
(-par), To mark with the tilak, to ornament; to anoint, install, consecrate, crown; to betroth; to bid adieu (to)

def: s.f. A short-sleeved (or a sleeveless) robe; a gown; a robe of honour ( = ḵẖilʻat).
s.f. A kind of necklace.
adj. & s.m. Spotted, freckled, marked with moles, &c.; chief, principal; — the fourth part of a barley-corn, the unit of length.
s.m. pl. (of tilm), Boys, servants.
s.m. A kind of song, &c.
s.m. = H تلنگي तिलंगी tilaṅgī, s.f. A kind of paper kite (cf. tuklā).
s.m. An inhabitant of Tailanga or Tilangāna (in which the first soldiers were clothed and disciplined in the European manner; hence), a soldier.
s.f. = H تلنگا तिलंगा tilaṅgā s.m. A kind of paper kite (cf. tuklā).
s.f. See s.v. til.
Having or consisting of three strings; a necklace, or any ornament, of three strings or chains.
Having or consisting of three strings; a necklace, or any ornament, of three strings or chains.
s.m. See s.v. til.
adj. Sloped towards the oil (the wick of a lamp).
s.m. = طلاوه t̤ilāwa, q.v.
s.m. corr. of t̤ilāya, q.v.

ety: for A. تلاوة, inf. n. of تلو
def: s.f. Reading (particularly the Qorʼān); meditation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تلاوت (tilāvat), taken from Arabic تِلَاوَة (tilāwa).

def: s.f. Name of a plain in the vicinity of Sirhind abounding in robbers (hence it is applied to any situation of danger).
adj. Golden; made of gold; covered with gold
subs: s.f. Painting or lettering with gold.

def: s.m. lit. 'Spotted or speckled'; the starling; a turtle dove, or other speckled bird ( = til-war; til-yar).
s.m. A cockroach (Blatta).
s.f. = tilā-dānī, q.v. s.v. tilā.
adj. Oily.
s.m. = ṭīlā, q.v.

ety: tilhā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Oiliness.
s.f. A wooden or iron bar or wire (as of a bird-cage, &c.); the calf (of the leg; written also tilī; tulī).
s.f. dim. of ṭīlā, q.v.
s.f. = tīlī, q.v.
s.f. = teliṇ, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. a late usage of the Gr. τέλεσμα
def: s.m. A talisman; enchantment, magic; a mystery; mystical devices or characters; an image (or other object) upon which such devices or characters are engraved or inscribed (contrived for the purpose of preserving from enchantment, or from a variety of evils, &c.).
s.m. Talismans; spells; — a talisman, &c. ( = t̤ilism).

r: t̤ilismātī, t̤ilasmātī, t̤ilismī, t̤ilasmī, t̤ilismāt, t̤ilism
ety: rel. n. fr. t̤ilismāt, and t̤ilism
def: adj. Talismanic; magical; mysterious, mystical, &c.

r: t̤ilismī, t̤ilasmī, t̤ilismātī, t̤ilasmātī, t̤ilismāt, t̤ilism
ety: rel. n. fr. t̤ilismāt, and t̤ilism
def: adj. Talismanic; magical; mysterious, mystical, &c.
s.f. A small fowl, a young hen, a pullet; (contempt.) a fat young woman.
s.m. A starling; a turtle dove, or other small speckled bird ( = til-bar, til-war).

ety: inf. n. ii of لقي 'to meet'
def: s.m. Meeting with; arriving at; falling upon, encountering (in battle, &c.); meeting, encounter; reprehension.
s.m. See s.v. til for compounds of which that word is the first member.
s.f. The spleen or milt
subs: s.m. Enlargement of the spleen ( = tap-tillī, or tāp-tillī, q.v.s.v. tāp).

def: s.m. A scholar, pupil, disciple.
adj. Sloped towards the oil (the wick of a lamp).
s.m. Water and oil, watery oil.
s.m. A flatterer, wheedler, cajoler.
s.m. A kind of sweetmeat made of ground sesamum and sugar (eaten, particularly by Hindūs, when the sun enters Capricorn).
s.m. A small knotted piece of wood.
s.m. A starling ( = tilbar, q.v.).

ety: old H., correl. of jim and jimi
def: adv. = taise.
ex: کیا تم مجھ سے محبت کرتی ہو؟
kyā tum mujh-se mohabbat kartī ho?
تم جیسے لوگوں کی ہی ضرورت ہے یہاں۔
tum jaise logõ kī hī zarūrat hai yahā̃.
People like you are needed here.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुम्हि (tumhi), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe), from Sanskrit युष्मान् (yuṣmān, acc. pl.) adopting the initial sounds of त्वम् (tvám, nom. sg.).

def: s.f. = ṭīm-ṭimāk; ṭīm-ṭām; qq.v.
To damp, moisten, wet.
s.m. Care (of), attention (to), looking (after); attendance (on the sick); regimen (of the sick); infirmity, sickness, indisposition; affliction, sorrow, grief; condolence, sympathy.
s.m. A kind of fabulous fish of an enormous size (said to be one hundred yojanas long)
subs: s.m. lit. 'Swallowing even the timi'; a large fabulous fish
s.m. 'Swallowing even the timiṅgil'; a large fabulous fish.

def: s.f. (local), A cinder; a spark.
s.m. See s.v. تمي timi.
s.m. Darkness, obscurity; total blindness (from an affection of the optic nerve); — a sort of aquatic plant; — adj. Dark, gloomy
subs: adj. & s.m. Dispelling darkness; — anything that dispels darkness, a dispeller of darkness.

def: s.m. A kind of pumpkin, Benincasa cerifera; — a water-melon.
part. adj. Moistened, moist, wet, damp; unmoved, unshaken; steady, fixed; quiet, still.
s.m. Heat, pungency, &c. ( = tigma, q.v.).
s.f. Resemblance, likeness, picture, portrait, image, effigy.
s.m. = H ٿيم ٿماك टीमटिमाक ṭīm-ṭimāk, s.f. Dress, show, ostentation, &c. = ṭīp-ṭāp, q.v.; brocade, kimkhob ( = P. kim-ḵẖẉāb).
s.m. Soft or gentle rain, drizzle; a soft sound; a sound.

ety: fr. the preceding; — āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Faint light, glimmer, twinkle, flicker.
s.f. = H ٿيم ٿام टीमटाम ṭīm-ṭām, s.m. Dress, show, ostentation, &c. = ṭīp-ṭāp, q.v.; brocade, kimkhob ( = P. kim-ḵẖẉāb).
v.n. To twinkle; to flicker (cf. tamtamānā).
v.n. To give a faint light, to glimmer, to twinkle, to flicker, to be at the last gasp.
s.f. = tri-muhānī, q.v.

ety: Prk. ताणं; S. त्यानि, acc. pl. neut. of base तद्
def: The formative, or base of the oblique cases, of the plural of the pron. so or taun, qq.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀡𑀽 (taṇū), from Sanskrit तनू (tanū́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tanúHs, from Proto-Indo-European *tenúh₂s, from *tenuh₂- (“body”).

def: adj. Three
subs: s.f. lit. 'Three and five'; altercation, contention, dispute, squabble; knavish tricks, arts, dodges
tīn-pāṅć lānā (-se), To dispute or quarrel (with); to try one's tricks (with), practise tricks (upon)
s.m. The number three; an odd number
s.m. The private parts; the penis
adj. Three and thirteen (regarded as unlucky numbers); scattered, dispersed, broken; distracted; ruined, destroyed; squandered, dissipated, wasted; — tīn-terah karnā, v.t. To scatter, disperse, &c.
tīn-tīn karke, adv. Three each; three at a time, by threes, in threes
s.m. 'The three (original) letters of laʻnatʼ; (hence) imprecation, abuse, scorn, censure; — tīn ḥarf bhejnā, v.n. To abuse, curse, &c.
s.m. The rule of three (in Arith.)
s.m. The past, the present, and the future; morning, noon, and evening ( = tri-kāl)
tīn kāne, s.m. Three aces, a throw of three with three dice, a tres
adj. The three, all three; — s.m. = tīn-thān, q.v.
s.f. The three periods of human life, viz. childhood, manhood, and old age
s.m. The three worlds, &c. = tri-lok, q.v.

s.m. The three kinds of trouble or suffering, viz. Daitik or that occasioned by the body; Bhavatik or that which arises from existence or contact with the world; and Daivik or that which comes from Providence (as calamity, &c.)
s.m. The Three persons of the Hindū triad, viz. Brahmā, Vishnu, and Śiva

s.m. The three kinds of human obligations, i.e. devarṇ or the obligations of a worshipper to deity; pitr̤i-r̤in or the obligation of ancestors to posterity; and r̤ishi-r̤in or the obligation owing to the r̤ishis
s.m. = tīn kāl and tri-kāl, qq.v.
s.m. The three qualities or constituents of nature and every existing thing (viz. sattva, rajas, and tamas, see tri-guṇ and guṇ)
s.m. = tīnoṅ bhavan, and tri-lok, q.v.

def: s.m.(?) Clay, earth, mud, mould, soil, sand.
adj. = tīnoṅ, q.v.s.v. tīn.
v.n. = tinaknā, q.v.
v.n. To prick, to throb, palpitate, flutter; to break into a passion, to flare up (at, -par).
adj. = tīnoṅ, q.v.s.v. tīn.

ety: for A. طينة, n. of un. fr. t̤īn
def: s.f. A piece of clay, &c.; — natural constitution or disposition, nature, temperament, disposition, genius
subs: s.f. A bad disposition; — adj. Of a bad disposition, ill-disposed.

def: s.m. = تندو tindu, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) A kind of vegetable, Diospyros melanoxylon.
s.m. A species of ebony, Diospyros glutinosa (from the fruit of which is obtained a kind of resin used in India as pitch for caulking vessels, &c.).
s.f. The resinous fruit of the ebony tree called tindu.
adj. Made of clay, earthy.
adj. = tīnoṅ, q.v.s.v. tīn.
s.m. The tree Dalbergia ougeinensis.
adj. = tīnoṅ, q.v.s.v. tīn.
s.m. Grass, straw; a blade of grass; a bit of the stalk of grass; a bit or scrap (of thread, paper, &c.); a mote, particle
subs: (or muṅh-meṅ) lenā, v.n. To make submission; to confess inferiority; to ask for quarter
v.n. To have not a scrap remaining, to be cleaned out (of money, &c.), to be robbed of everything
v.n. 'To pick straws'; to be intoxicated, or insane, or mad, &c.

def: s.f. A blade of grass; a slight or frail thing or creature, a little or wee thing.
s.f. = tinnak, q.v.
s.f. A kind of rice.
s.m. A large serpent; a dragon; a great sea-monster, the great sea-serpent
subs: s.m. The constellation of the Dragon.

def: s.f. The plant Convolvulus turpethum (syn. turbud; teʼoṛī).
s.f. The tamarind tree.

s.f. The act of pressing or compressing; raising the voice (in singing); the highest pitch of the voice, a high or shrill note; drawing or winning (a card); — a bond (on unstamped paper, cf. tamassuk), a bill, a note of hand, promissory note; a cheque
subs: s.f. The act of pressing, &c. ( = ṭīp); — ornament, decoration; pomp, show, ostentation, splendour, magnificence (syn. ḵẖud-numāʼī).

def: s.f. Trivet, tripod; small table with three legs, a teapoy.
s.m. = tipāʼī, q.v.
s.f. The Cape gooseberry or Brazil cherry, Physalis peruviana; (the term tipārī is peculiar to Bengal; in north-western India the fruit is called mako).
adj. Having three leaves; (Bot.) ternate.
s.m. A drop; a spot; a stain (of any colour applied by the finger); the focus of a burning glass; (cf. ṭapkā, and ṭīkā).
v.t. lit. 'To cause to drop or exude'; to press, compress, squeeze; — to feel, to grope; — to sing soprano; to pocket (money); to note down, to register; to draw or win (a card)
subs: v.t. intens. of and = ṭīpnā, q.v.

def: s.m. (dialec.) Vaunting, boasting; boast (cf. ṭippas, and ṭīp)
s.f. Annotation, gloss, commentary ( = ṭīkā).
s.f. Annotation, commentary, &c. ( = ṭippan).
s.m. Firm footing; ground, foundation; claim; — adherence, union, alliance, coalition; — conceit, pride, haughtiness, arrogance ( = ṭhasak)
subs: ṭippas lagānā, v.n. To obtain a firm footing; to lay a foundation; to secure the interest (of); to put in a word for oneself; to make out a claim.

def: s.m. Shore, bank; margin, brink, edge; — postpn. & adv. Near (to, -ke), close at hand
subs: s.m. The province of Tirhut, lying to the north of Bihār
tīraj, adj. & s.m. Growing or standing near a shore; — a tree near a shore.

def: s.f. The fourth solar month of the Persian year (corresponding to the Hindī Sāwan); the festival held on the third day of the lunar fortnight of the month Sāwan ( = tīj)

ety: Old P. tigra; Zend tighri, rt. tij = S. तिज्; S. तीर, prob. fr. P.
def: s.m. An arrow; — a beam, a mast; — the planet Mercury ( = tīr-ě-falak)
subs: s.m. A shower of arrows; a punishment in which the culprit is shot with arrows
adj. lit. 'An arrow to the mark'; hitting the mark; to the point
s.m. The range of an arrow, bow-shot distance
tīr ćalānā, tīr ćhoṛnā, v.n. To shoot an arrow; (iron.) to do something wonderful, to do great things
tīr-ḵẖākī, s.f. A light, small-headed arrow
adj. & s.m. Struck or wounded by an arrow; — one so struck, &c.
s.m. The range of an arrow, bowshot distance
adj. & adv. Dart-like; as by an arrow
s.f. The piercing of the body by an arrow
s.m. The range of an arrow, &c. ( = tīr-ras, q.v.)
v.t. To put out of sight; to hide; to make away with
s.m. A quiver; an embrasure; an aperture, a loop-hole
(-meṅ, -ko), To be struck or hit by an arrow (a mark or other object)
s.m. A viper; a viper's teeth
(-ko), To strike (an object) with an arrow; to shoot an arrow (at an object), &c. = tīr ćalānā, q.v.
s.m. An arrow discharged through a tube
v.t. To do to death with arrows
v.t. To put to a lingering death, to kill by degrees, to torture.

def: s.f. A transverse piece in cloths, a breadth cut diagonally.
